28-Day Product Creation Formula
28 Days From Conception To Cash
Week 2: Days 8-10
THE BOOK AND VIDEO FORMULA
Creating Your Info-Product Outline
The outline that follows is what I use to quickly create books, e-books,
video tutorials or any other digital info-style product
This format is used to help organize your data quickly, as well as help
the user quickly digest your data, thus reducing refunds
False Belief #1: “Books Must Be Long.”
False Belief #2: “Books Must Be Complete.”
False Belief #3: “Books Must Be Written By Writers.”

-- 1 of 26 --

28-Day Product Creation Formula
28 Days From Conception To Cash
Week 2: Days 8-10
THE BOOK AND VIDEO FORMULA
Creating Your Info-Product Outline
You can write as you go, or simply create the outline using bullet points,
rough ideas, and Googling information pertaining to the subject.
The idea here is to get the outline down. We will refine the outline and
fill in the blanks on days 11-14.

-- 2 of 26 --

28-Day Product Creation Formula
28 Days From Conception To Cash
Week 2: Days 8-10
SECTION OUTLINE
FOREWORD (Optional)
Many product authors get caught up in having a big name write a killer
foreword for them.
This is unnecessary, but if you CAN have this done, it will make for a
'slightly' more professional-looking product.
Don't get hung up in it. Simply ask an expert in the field to pen a 1-page
foreword, and you’re set.

-- 3 of 26 --

28-Day Product Creation Formula
28 Days From Conception To Cash
Week 2: Days 8-10
INTRODUCTION
Your introduction serves three purposes:
1.
Re-emphasize and fully expound on the reader’s problem.
2.
Touch on the big-picture solution, but do so by future-pacing.
3.
Let the reader know what to expect.

-- 4 of 26 --

28-Day Product Creation Formula
28 Days From Conception To Cash
Week 2: Days 8-10
THE 3-SECTION STRUCTURE: OVERVIEW
Think of your book as a movie or a television series. Most movies and TV
shows have three 'acts'. So should your book or product.
Section 1
An extended version of your Introduction. (4 mini-chapters)
Section 2
Information and Application Section (4 mini-chapters)
Section 3
Tips, Tricks and Tactics Section (4 mini-chapters)

-- 5 of 26 --

28-Day Product Creation Formula
28 Days From Conception To Cash
Week 2: Days 8-10
THE 3-SECTION STRUCTURE: OVERVIEW
Number of pages is irrelevant.
Simply make sure you follow the Formula outlined and put in the best quality
information you can find.
You can use this Formula to literally create a 20-page book if you want…or a
200-page book. It’s totally up to you.

-- 6 of 26 --

28-Day Product Creation Formula
28 Days From Conception To Cash
Week 2: Days 8-10
THE 3-SECTION STRUCTURE: SECTION 1
Essentially a longer and reformatted version of your Introduction, with more
facts and figures
Creative Tip
Begin each chapter within each section with a
quote from someone famous
Advanced Creative Tip
Elaborate on this quote by starting off each chapter with a short story. This can
be 1-2 pages tops, and is used to engage the reader’s imagination before
diving into facts. (Tim Ferriss’ Article/Chapter Tip)

-- 7 of 26 --

28-Day Product Creation Formula
28 Days From Conception To Cash
Week 2: Days 8-10
THE 3-SECTION STRUCTURE: SECTION 1
Chapter 1: You Can Do This
Not the title, but the concept.
Here you emphazise the fact that anyone with desire can get X done,
and X will make your life super awesome.
Chapter 2: Here’s Why You Must Do This
Here you re-emphasize the DIRE need to take action. Elaborate on the
materials from the introduction, and introduce new data that let's them know
this is really, really important. Then, within this chapter, remind them that their
life will, again, be super awesome once they get X handled.

-- 8 of 26 --

28-Day Product Creation Formula
28 Days From Conception To Cash
Week 2: Days 8-10
THE 3-SECTION STRUCTURE: SECTION 1
Chapter 3: Why X Has Been So Confusing Up Until Now
You create a feeling of inclusion when you say, "X was confusing to me, and in
ways it still is. Good teachers are always learning. It used to bother me that X
was so confusing, but once I found out what I'm about to share with you, I
began to realize that I wasn't as much to blame as were the teachers/books/
method of learning prior to reading your book was.”

-- 9 of 26 --

28-Day Product Creation Formula
28 Days From Conception To Cash
Week 2: Days 8-10
THE 3-SECTION STRUCTURE: SECTION 1
Chapter 4: Your Life After X
Here you paint a vivid picture, again, of what the reader's life will look like after
applying what you're sharing.
Paint this picture in 3 ways:
1. Future Pacing
2. Success Stories
3. Your Personal Story

-- 10 of 26 --

28-Day Product Creation Formula
28 Days From Conception To Cash
Week 2: Days 8-10
THE 3-SECTION STRUCTURE: SECTION 2
Section 2 is your Information And Application Section. Here, you outline the
basic idea behind your plan for success in X, and always let the user know that
specifics are forthcoming (That's Section 3.)
Creative Tip
Begin each chapter within each section with a
quote from someone famous
Advanced Creative Tip
Elaborate on this quote by starting off each chapter with a short story. This can
be 1-2 pages tops, and is used to engage the reader’s imagination before
diving into facts. (Tim Ferriss’ Article/Chapter Tip)

-- 11 of 26 --

28-Day Product Creation Formula
28 Days From Conception To Cash
Week 2: Days 8-10
THE 3-SECTION STRUCTURE: SECTION 2
Chapter 1: X Overview
Here you want to overview your solution to X, with an emphasis on structure
(whether over days, weeks or months) and on application tips.
Think of this as the outline of your "Blueprint For X." The blueprint will be the
step-by-step, 'do this next' outline, covered in the following chapter.
Again, pre-awareness of material creates a more comfortable learning
experience, and opens the mind for the materials to come.

-- 12 of 26 --

28-Day Product Creation Formula
28 Days From Conception To Cash
Week 2: Days 8-10
THE 3-SECTION STRUCTURE: SECTION 2
Chapter 1: X Overview
This is an ideal chapter to cover big-picture X Structure, saying things like,
"Don't worry about the details of (whatever you're discussing.) We'll cover that
in (chapter here.)"
This will create what I call "User Read-Through." The user is pushed forward
with every page and encouraged to keep reading. In a sense, you're selling
your idea/solution in a compelling way to ensure the reader will take action, and
be excited to do so.

-- 13 of 26 --

28-Day Product Creation Formula
28 Days From Conception To Cash
Week 2: Days 8-10
THE 3-SECTION STRUCTURE: SECTION 2
Chapter 2: The X Preparation Kit
You want to have a short chapter on the things the user will need to go through
the X Plan. I call this the "Toolkit”, or "Ingredients For Success."
1. Mental Prep (emphasize that motivation is key, and reference sources)
2. Physical Prep, if any.
3. Any materials the reader will require.
4. Any time-management tools the reader may require.
5. Financial Prep (if needed)
6. Emotional Prep (important; what challenges might he/she face?)
7. Spiritual Prep (if applicable)

-- 14 of 26 --

28-Day Product Creation Formula
28 Days From Conception To Cash
Week 2: Days 8-10
THE 3-SECTION STRUCTURE: SECTION 2
Chapter 3: The X Plan
Here is where you describe specifics of your plan, broken down into as many
bite-size chunks as possible.
Make the plan as simplistic as possible, and remind the user that "genuis
demands simplicity.”

-- 15 of 26 --

28-Day Product Creation Formula
28 Days From Conception To Cash
Week 2: Days 8-10
THE 3-SECTION STRUCTURE: SECTION 2
Chapter 4: What You Can Expect From X
One of the most overlooked aspects of products is a dedicated chapter to what
the user can expect. Preparing them in advance for what to expect, both
rewarding and challenging in nature, will inspire the reader to follow through.
Most people are not afraid of a challenge—they're afraid of the unknown.
Making both the good, rewarding aspects of your product known AND the
challenges they will face (and how to overcome them) removes uncertainty.
Ideal section for an FAQ

-- 16 of 26 --

28-Day Product Creation Formula
28 Days From Conception To Cash
Week 2: Days 8-10
THE 3-SECTION STRUCTURE: SECTION 3
Section 3 is your Tips, Tricks and Tactics Section. This should be positioned as
shortcuts for beginners and advanced tips for those already experienced in X.
Save your most "ninja" ideas for this section.
Creative Tip
Begin each chapter within each section with a
quote from someone famous
Advanced Creative Tip
Elaborate on this quote by starting off each chapter with a short story. This can
be 1-2 pages tops, and is used to engage the reader’s imagination before
diving into facts. (Tim Ferriss’ Article/Chapter Tip)

-- 17 of 26 --

28-Day Product Creation Formula
28 Days From Conception To Cash
Week 2: Days 8-10
THE 3-SECTION STRUCTURE: SECTION 3
Chapter 1: The X Ninja
Delve into advanced tricks and tactics used by "some of the most outrageously
successful (TOPIC) people I've ever seen." Here you can really let loose and cover wilder
ideas, more advanced techniques, and so on.
Chapter 2: Top 10 Tips For X Success
Try to give famous examples of each, or at least testimonials (real or parable-style) after
giving each tip.
For example, you don't just want to say, "Set your alarm 30 minutes ahead of time to
jump-start your day," and move on from there. State the tip, then tell a story about how a
specific person or persons used that tip to become wildly successful at X. This can be
one of the more enjoyable chapters to write!

-- 18 of 26 --

28-Day Product Creation Formula
28 Days From Conception To Cash
Week 2: Days 8-10
THE 3-SECTION STRUCTURE: SECTION 3
Chapter 3: The Most Common Mistakes People Make With X
List the most common mistakes people make here when going through your program
specifically. If no one has, you'll have to use parable-style writing and imagine what they
would be. Then, give the reader tips on how to avoid these mistakes, and, if they DO commit
them, let them know that they are still on the path to success IF they choose to learn from
their mistakes.
Use This Questionnaire:
1. Did you make this mistake? (Yes/No)
2. If so, what was the most painful part of making this mistake for you personally? (A few lines for them to write.)
3. Did this mistake cause any of your friends or family experience pain as well? (A few lines for them to write.)
4. As someone dedicated to success, jot down what you learned from this. (A few lines for them to write.)
5. What are 3 ideas you plan to implement to make sure this mistake is avoided in the future? (Three lines.)

-- 19 of 26 --

28-Day Product Creation Formula
28 Days From Conception To Cash
Week 2: Days 8-10
THE 3-SECTION STRUCTURE: SECTION 3
Chapter 4: Conclusion
Wrap up your thoughts, leaving the reader with absolute assurance that, if they follow your
program, they too can see success in X. Include 2-3 testimonial stories here if possible. End
on a very positive, strong note, and include any pertinent contact information they may need.

-- 20 of 26 --

28-Day Product Creation Formula
28 Days From Conception To Cash
Week 2: Days 11-14
REFINING AND EDITING YOUR OUTLINE
Divide And Conquer
Now is the time to break your outline into specific chapters, come up with names for these
chapters (don't worry about being too clever; clarity is key), and divide those chapters into
mini-chapters (sections within each chapter.)
You can add more information to each section and chapter as well. Feel free to literally write
each one, or just give yourself enough information to be able to record your outline and
turn that into your product (covered in Week 3).
Bare Minimum: Outline the ideas you would cover as ifyou were giving this as a speech in
front of a live audience without a teleprompter.

-- 21 of 26 --

28-Day Product Creation Formula
28 Days From Conception To Cash
Week 2: Days 11-14
REFINING AND EDITING YOUR OUTLINE
Specifics
Use this time to add specific dates, research specifics, etc. You'll also want to outline your
stories (i.e. parables, testimonies, etc) in a simple fashion, like:
1. Name of person
2. Something unique about that person. ("Sally had the most amazing smile I've ever seen.
She would enter a room, and everyone would just get instantly happier." Notice this might not
have anything to do with the actual issue of the parable, but it makes it more reader-friendly
and lends to credibility.)
3. That person's experience with X problem/challenge/whatever.
4. An ending quote from that person after going through it. This is usually something positive.

-- 22 of 26 --

28-Day Product Creation Formula
28 Days From Conception To Cash
Week 2: Days 11-14
REFINING AND EDITING YOUR OUTLINE
Your goal at the end of Day 14 is to have a document that is outlined to the point to
where all you would need to do is start speaking into a recording device and you
would end up with a product several hours later.
